title: rosa coriifolia subsp. entraunensis
intro: Rosa coriifolia subsp. entraunensis, also known as the wild rose or Rosa coriifolia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly found in various parts of the world. The medicinal properties of Rosa coriifolia subsp. entraunensis include its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Rosa coriifolia subsp. entraunensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health concerns.
